items.title: VI Live: Emegha-loos Strasbourg knikkert AS Monaco uit Coupe de France
items.summary: Donderdagavond staat de laatste wedstrijd in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. Telstar neemt het om 20.00 uur op tegen Go Ahead Eagles. Ook in het buitenland is er bekervoetbal. Blijf op de hoogte in de VI Live.

items.title: Linthorst begrijpt fans die bus opwachten bij Eagles: 'Maar we geven alles'
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles werd door Telstar uitgeschakeld in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker en dat was een nieuwe domper voor de ploeg uit Deventer. De ploeg draait alles behalve lekker in de competitie en dat leverde al boze reacties op van de fans, die de bus opwachtten om verhaal te halen bij de spelers en trainer. Na afloop sprak verslaggever Kalum van Oudheusden met een ontgoochelde Evert Linthorts.

items.title: Saudi league warns Ronaldo over transfer protest
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has been told by the Saudi Pro League that "no individual -- however significant -- determines decisions beyond their own club" amid a dispute over transfer spending.

items.title: Leicester deducted 6 points for P&S rule breach
items.summary: Championship side Leicester City have been deducted six points after being found to have breached the EFL's profit and sustainability rules for the 2023/24 season.

items.title: Lookman stars in debut as Atleti make Copa semi
items.summary: Ademola Lookman scored on his Atlético Madrid debut in a 5-0 rout of Real Betis on Thursday that sent Diego Simeone's team to the semifinals of the Copa del Rey.

items.title: Benzema scores debut hat trick in Al Hilal rout
items.summary: Karim Benzema made an instant impact following his contentious move to Al Hilal, scoring a hat trick on his debut for the Saudi Pro League club.

items.title: Source: Minnesota finalizing James Rodríguez deal
items.summary: Minnesota United is finalizing a deal to sign Colombia international star forward James Rodríguez, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Atlético sloopt Betis met vijfklapper: Hancko én debutant Lookman scoren
items.summary: Atlético Madrid heeft een bijzonder fijne avond beleefd in de Copa del Rey. In de kwartfinale werd Real Betis met liefst 0-5 uitgeschakeld. Glansrollen waren er voor Dávid Hancko en basisdebutant Ademola Lookman.

items.title: Pijnlijke conclusie na bekeruitschakeling Go Ahead: 'Alles ging mis'
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles bevindt zich in een diep dal. De Deventenaren strandden donderdag in de kwartfinale van het KNVB Bekertoernooi tegen Telstar en wachten al sinds 9 november 2025 op een overwinning na negentig minuten. Aanvoerder Joris Kramer trok een pijnlijke conclusie.

items.title: De Roon knikkert Koopmeiners en co hardhandig uit de Italiaanse beker
items.summary: Atalanta is donderdagavond met groots machtsvertoon doorgedrongen tot de halve finale van de Coppa Italia. Juventus, waar Teun Koopmeiners inviel, werd met ruime cijfers verslagen.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo warned by Saudi Pro League amid transfer spending dispute
items.summary: Al-Nassr forward reportedly unhappy with transfer policy ‘No one determines decisions beyond their own club’ Cristiano Ronaldo has been told by the Saudi Pro League that “no individual – however significant – determines decisions beyond their own club” amid a dispute over transfer spending. The Portuguese superstar, who turned 41 on Thursday, is believed to be unhappy with Al-Nassr’s lack of activity in the January transfer window. Continue reading...

items.title: Injury scare for England's Feyi-Waboso ahead of Six Nations opener
items.summary: Immanuel Feyi-Waboso has given England an injury scare ahead of their Six Nations opener against Wales after failing to finish training on Thursday.

items.title: Saudi league warns Ronaldo amid doubts over future
items.summary: The Saudi Pro League warns Cristiano Ronaldo "no individual" determines decisions outside of his club amid doubts over his future at Al-Nassr.

items.title: Bad Bunny on Super Bowl half-time show: 'Be proud of who you are'
items.summary: Bad Bunny has vowed to make the world happy and to make the world dance when he makes his historic appearance at the Super Bowl 60 half-time show on Sunday.

items.title: Celtic chairman calls for unity over fan boycott plans
items.summary: Celtic interim chairman Brian Wilson calls for "unity behind the team" amid plans from some fans to boycott Saturday's Scottish Cup match against Dundee.

items.title: Does spending £60m on a young player pay off in the Premier League?
items.summary: Liverpool paid £60m for the 20-year-old defender Jérémy Jacquet. Previous deals in the league suggest it is a risk By WhoScored As Premier League teams get younger and transfer fees get bigger, we are seeing players with little experience commanding huge prices. Jérémy Jacquet’s £60m move to Liverpool makes him the fourth most expensive player aged 20 or younger in Premier League history. Spending so much money for a defender with just 36 appearances in France’s top flight looks like a risk, but how have players Jacquet’s age fared after big moves? Continue reading...

items.title: 'Makes me sick' - how Tom Brady created a Super Bowl storm
items.summary: Tom Brady may not be playing in the Super Bowl, but he remains a topic of discussion as the New England Patriots prepare to face the Seattle Seahawks this Sunday.

items.title: Seahawks star expected to play at Super Bowl after injury scare
items.summary: The Seattle Seahawks are expected to have Nick Emmanwori available for Sunday's Super Bowl despite a late injury scare for the star defender.

items.title: Frank says Romero ‘dealt with internally’ for latest volley at Spurs owners
items.summary: Manager refuses to criticise captain for outburst ‘Too much attention’ given to social media post Thomas Frank has refused to criticise Cristian Romero after the defender’s social media attack on Tottenham’s owners, with the Argentina inter­national remaining club captain. The Spurs manager was on the defensive in his press conference before Saturday’s Premier League ­fixture at Manchester United. He would not give any steer as to the outcome of Romero’s outburst – the second in two months – only saying “we have dealt with the matter internally”. Continue reading...

items.title: France clinch bonus-point Six Nations victory as Ireland pay for abject 50 minutes
items.summary: France got their Six Nations title defence off to the ideal start as they put a sorry Ireland to the sword with a 36-14 victory at the Stade de France in Paris.
